title: Weak pointers use a color-disambiguated caret-question suffix
summary: std::weak_ptr<T> displays as T^?, using a neutral caret for non-owning pointer identity and a bold gold question mark for possible absence.
details: Unique and shared pointers continue to display as T^, with ownership semantics carried by mutation-red and copy-gold carets respectively.
The weak caret is neutral gray and the question mark uses the same selected gold accent as optional and expected semantics.
The owner explicitly accepts symbol overlap: color information encodes the semantic distinction between owning, shared, weak, and optional roles.
The recursive grammar applies this spelling uniformly in constructor parameters, declarations, returns, lambdas, arrays, ordinary containers, and nested optional/expected combinations.
Minimal parentheses prevent actual spelling collisions across nested source types: optional<weak_ptr<T>> is (T^?)?, while weak_ptr<optional<T>> is T?^?.
The selected style-lab catalog hash is d590b72d38e937964e3ccd006dfcf12cd65b841705d3c3174e9c38d3b353eead.
rationale: Caret preserves the established pointer-like visual language.
Question mark communicates that locking a weak pointer may produce no target.
Semantic color already carries distinctions elsewhere in the frontend, so a new tilde or literal w is unnecessary.
alternatives: T~? gives non-ownership a distinct glyph; rejected because the selected color grammar makes the extra symbol distinction unnecessary.
T^w labels weakness literally; rejected in favor of the established question-mark absence semantics.

provenance.note: After requesting the question a second time, the owner selected caret-optional and stated that symbol overlap is acceptable because colors encode semantic differences.
Buffer-local candidate profiles feed the shared recursive grammar and semantic highlight roles.

evidence.claim: Real ext_linegrid captures pin candidate text, equal widths, neutral heads, gold tails, and source integrity.
Direct and nested weak types are covered across 25 wrapper pairs, a 125-row rendered corpus, the reported constructor, edit cycles, Visual reveal, and module toggles.

questions: What do the two colors in T^? mean?
Why does weak_ptr reuse caret and question-mark symbols?

last_verified.method: Owner's second style-lab selection and rationale captured, then pinned with real-grid RGB and generated interaction coverage.
